transcript.text: Here we go.
No worries.
I've done it before myself.
Sorry about that.
I had to get in transit from the office back home, and it was one of those.
So I appreciate the flexibility.
Yeah, no worries.
How's the week been?
Oh, my gosh.
So incredibly busy.
How's the start of the new year for you?
It's nuts, but it's a power.
Everything's changing in this space every 10 minutes.
So I'm sure you guys are deeply aware.
It's insane.
Like, you can't keep up.
There's just no way.
Yeah, and we're on the data side of it.
So it's funny because everyone else is changing objectives, and we have to change our products.
And our products change slower because we're doing the underlying data that's powering the products.
So it's always trying to meet expectations because the front-end stuff changes faster than what we do, unfortunately.
Like, we have to set up active collection and do all these steps, and you can imagine.
you.
Yeah, no, absolutely.
I feel that.
And yeah, I mean, engineering can build a lot faster, which is great.
But yeah, it's making decisions in real time.
It's wild.
Yep.
So that being said, thank you for signing the NDA, so we can get into it.
So in addition to our typical clickstream data points, we do have a set of prompt and response data, probably the largest in the world.
I don't know what the competition is for this.
I think we're the only people in the space.
There's some smaller players, but we're sitting on a set, about 80 million real user prompts and responses a month, give or take sample size changes.
So that's what we need an NDA to say, is that we do have underlying user prompt and response data.
Yeah.
And I think that's the unique part that, you know, a lot of people-
People are doing just the proxies, you know, just with the probes through the APIs and, you know, getting a response, and it's just kind of guesswork.
That's, you know, we don't know what people are actually searching for, and, you know, you can create those prompts through keyword searches, but it's not, again, it's just guesswork, right?
Exactly, yeah, we have the real user inputs, typos and all, and I assure you, if you're used to looking at keyword data, wait a second, start looking at prompt data, because it's what you'd imagine.
Well, yeah, I mean, when I search myself, like, I catch myself, and I'll analyze what I just typed, I was like, oh gosh, yeah, like, I mean, that's not what you would expect, you know?
So if you're writing a prompt, trying to guess.
But people, real people are actually querying.
They're not going to get it right.
Yes.
That's a big data set.
It's huge.
Yeah.
We are the major player in the background of the GEO space.
We don't market it.
We only talk about it under NDA because it's so sensitive and such a competitive place.
But if you see all these tools spinning up, we're usually involved.
Okay.
Okay.
So I did have some questions about what – so it's great that you guys have the actuals, but what do you provide your data partners, like us?
Would it be the actual response data or would it be aggregated metrics based on topic?
Like, how are you – like, what are you providing?
So that's the beauty of having the raw data.
I could just reverse it.
What are you guys interested in?
Because we can model – this is a custom data set for us.
So this is in some API that we can turn on.
We have a data science team that manages this, and we can configure it in whatever way.
Oh, okay.
So you have all this historic data you're saying?
Yeah, these are all custom engagements.
So we can configure it with whatever is feasible for the team's needs.
I do want to caveat that we do charge a premium for this.
I think I told you last time.
for sure.
And what was the – because it was like an estimate.
You mentioned six figures, but – For raw data, seven.
For aggregated, mid-six.
Okay.
Anything raw is going to be seven figures just because of competitive risk.
Yeah, and that's per year.
And then aggregated, six.
Got it.
A lot of variance in the aggregation.
A lot of variance.
Because if you want everything, it's going to be very expensive.
But if you want shoes and smartwatches, we can be a lot more creative and flexible there.
Mm-hmm.
Okay.
Okay.
That makes a ton of sense.
And And –
So what AI engines do you support or have data for?
We have all of the major ones.
So we don't have Sparky and Rufus yet, which is the insight of itself that no one's using Sparky.
Nobody cares about us.
Yeah, it's really funny.
Everyone's like, why don't you have that?
I'm like, that's the insight is that we don't have it.
But we have ChatGPT, we have Gemini, we have Perplexity.
We're working on Cloud.
I don't think Cloud would be relevant for this use case, but we're working on it.
No, no.
I don't think most of our CMOs that we talk to are caring about that.
But they do.
I mean, obviously, like the AI overviews, whether it's like Bing or Google, that's relevant.
We've heard, yeah, we've pretty much just heard that, like those is the ones that you mentioned.
And then the AI overviews is the standard.
heard it.
That's
Haven't heard of any smaller ones that, you know, people, like, are asking for.
Yeah.
Just to specify that, we have AI mode.
AI overviews, we could theoretically get.
No one's paid us to store that yet.
Oh, okay.
So you're not actually doing the, like, scraping, basically, AI overviews and stuff.
We could.
That would just be, that'd be an enormous data storage task.
So we'd need someone.
sure, yeah.
If we were, because we're looking at a sample of 1% to 3% of digital population.
So storing all of that would be a lot.
yeah.
So how did you say you're getting that instead?
Sorry.
Oh, AI mode, we get through the same methodology we're getting from ChatGPT.
I have be careful on exec methodology just because of competitive positioning.
But the gist of it is we can tell when an API is triggered.
Yeah, okay.
And our panel, we can see when the ChatGPT API is triggered, and then we can capture the user input.
Yeah.
And.
All that's changing all the time.
I don't know if you opt in to any of, like, the, you know, Google's experimental stuff.
And I'm just like, I just got used to this one thing.
You're to probably change it on me.
And so, yeah, I can imagine your guys' job is really, really difficult to stay on top of that.
It's fun.
So, you have historical data, got the engines you support.
I will, I do want a caveat, though.
So, even though we cover most engines, the volume is on ChatGPT and Gemini is picking up.
But the majority of the volume of data you're going to see is on ChatGPT and Gemini.
So, even though we do have different engines, it's heavily ChatGPT skewed.
And skewed is the word.
It's just, there's more users on ChatGPT, so therefore we have more data.
Yeah, that makes sense.
So, my, sorry.
Sorry.
Oh, no, no, no, for sure.
was just looking at my questions, making sure I got the core ones.
Yeah, I think because of understanding that you guys actually have the real responses, the actuals, I don't need to ask the quality questions that I wanted to.
Yeah, and just the background, we actually stumbled into this just because we already had the panel built, and we have the largest panel in the world for our traditional business.
So this just became something we were able to do because we already had a panel built and waited.
So we didn't have, that's why we were able to do this, because we had our panel built for estimating web traffic.
And then this just turned out to be something else we could do with that panel build.
Yeah, definitely.
Yeah, so I think like licensing is one of the things that I wanted to, I mean, you know, I know it was like, yes to licensing, but I remember that there were tighter constraints.
For the AI visibility data, did I remember that correctly?
So I hate that I'm going to have to answer a bunch of questions, and it depends, but I'm about to answer a bunch of questions.
So with these type of deals, I have to get C-suite approval.
That's just the reality of it, because we do have an AI product.
We're playing both sides.
Yeah, yeah, yeah.
So we do have an AI product.
So I'm probably going to to – it might be easier to just do this via email, but I'm going to send you some questions, basically.
How do you guys plan to incorporate this data, front-end or back-end?
So when I say front-end, do you guys plan to showing raw prompts to your users?
Because my C-suite is probably going to say no to that, just because there's a lot of security concerns and a lot of things that go wrong if we start publishing raw prompts out to the general public.
Questions like that.
We're also going to get asked, basically, how you guys plan to use this, and if you guys prefer a raw or modeled approach.
Typically, people aim for a model to
Because while the raw data is super powerful, we've done this before, so we can do the modeling for you and save you guys a few dev cycles and some cost on licensing as well.
So that's typically the option most people opt for.
We have done raw engagements before with similar companies.
I will be honest, those work out very well.
It just takes usually a six-month implementation cycle to model the raw data correctly.
I saw that modeling huge amounts of text data is one of our specialties, and it's hard to do.
So those are usually what we're going do.
In terms of constrictions, I just have to get approval to make sure it's not a complete competitive threat.
And I need to get, basically, we need to narrow in on scope.
So from your perspective, do you see this as you guys just seeing share of topics from us, share of brands?
Like, what, from this data set, if you hypothetically had it all, what would be the insights you wanted to extract?
So I think that, you know...
We're doing a little of this on our own, but just not, you know, not the actuals and not the sheer, you know, volume at all.
And so, yeah, I, you know, I'd have to think about it because I'd have to think about whether it be, like, a replacement for what we're doing or an augmentation for what we're doing.
Because I can see there's, like, a lot of value in some augmentation, but also it's, there could be some opportunities just to replace.
Because, like you said, it is, you know, there's a reason that you're charging this much.
It is costly.
And it is hard to...
is to...
Get it, you know, the right data, accurate data and stuff.
So, yeah, I'll have to take this back to my CTO and my CEO and kind of figure out if there's some opportunity to do something with the aggregated data or if we would be actuals, the raw data.
Because, again, I'm not sure, like, if we want to just augment what we're doing or completely replace.
Yeah.

From my experience, when we do estimates and augmented, we can save about three months of legal cycle, to be completely honest with you, because lawyers love to, it's hard to get the raw data through legal sometimes, just from my experience.
Yeah.
That's my recommended approach, at least to start.
Okay.
So, if you want a, if you guys want to set up a call, I can bring on one of my counterparts.
I work with a counterpart who's
One of the experts in this world.
I mean, we're one of the people who have it, but he's happy to talk to you and your CTO with the exact nuances of this data, ways it can be modeled, whether that's in volume or in shares.
We can talk through all of that, if that's what you'd prefer.
Yeah.
We're heads down on a couple, we have a couple of different major projects in flight right now.

So it might be a couple weeks, but I'll take this information back to them and, you know, given it, depending upon their schedules, like they have, you know, board meeting next week, you know how it goes, right?
Um, and so it might be a couple weeks, um, but I'll definitely get back to you and definitely email me those questions too.
Yeah.
And we totally understand these, these type of things don't happen overnight.
This usually takes quarters.
Yeah, for sure.
Hopefully we can do it in quarter, but not quarter.
No, I know.
I mean, I like prior to this.

This company ever didn't, you know, longer engagements, no self-serve, no agency type of scenarios, like B2B Enterprise, where it'd be like year-long engagements, like when I was at DataRobot and stuff like that.
So, yeah, definitely.
Is DataRobot a partner of ours?
I think they are.
I don't know.
They've changed since I left there.
They've changed.
They really pivoted.
I think, yeah, I mean, they partner with every, you know, like they are really, really good about, they're really good partners, I think.
Like, I mean, they're really, they do great co-marketing.
They do, you know, they're really fantastic partners in my experience.
But, yeah, they really pivoted to the agency or to the agent-gentic side of things and pivoted away from the auto ML side of things.
So, that's been kind of interesting to see because I don't have access to the product anymore.
So I can't see what's under the hood.
I have some friends that still work there, though.
So I need to ask them what's actually happening there.
Yeah.
And just to share the correct numbers, you can float over to the C-suite.
So we're 80 million prompts a month worldwide, all languages.
So that is the number we are collecting.
Some of those are code blocks.
I do want to be honest.
So that might not be relevant.
But that is the number.
Okay, great.
Well, English, US, we're about 8 million a month, which is still very strong.
Not including code blocks.
So that's the type of coverage we're looking at.
On ChatGPT only.
Okay.
All right.
And yeah, you said the majority was ChatGPT.
Yes.
We have other engines, but in reality, 70% of the usage is coming from ChatGPT.
So that's where our data collection is.
Yeah, that makes sense.
All righty.
I think I have most of the information I need.
And again.
I think
Yeah, email me those questions.
That would be helpful to prepare for, you know, if we want to do another meeting with you guys.
But I've got lots of information to take back.
Yep, perfect.
And just don't tell everyone, know we're already under a DA, but we're very secretive about this line of the business.
Yes, yes.
Understandable.
Yeah, for sure.
Awesome.
Well, thanks so much for your time, Stevie.
I really appreciate it.
And look forward to reconnecting.
I'll shoot you an email with the information I need to get C-suite approval.
And hopefully we connect in the next few weeks.
All right.
Well, thanks.
Take care.
You as well.
Bye.
Bye.
Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[To explore a potential data partnership for Similarweb's AI prompt data.](https://fathom.video/share/w24K97Mf6mHqpviLmPa_iufLHuVyr2ZQ?tab=summary&timestamp=78.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Unique Data Asset:** Similarweb collects \~80M real user prompts/mo (8M US English) from a panel, providing authentic, un-guessed user intent.](https://fathom.video/share/w24K97Mf6mHqpviLmPa_iufLHuVyr2ZQ?tab=summary&timestamp=82.0)
  - [**Customizable Data:** The data is delivered via custom engagements, not a standard API, and can be raw or modeled to fit specific needs.](https://fathom.video/share/w24K97Mf6mHqpviLmPa_iufLHuVyr2ZQ?tab=summary&timestamp=207.0)
  - [**Pricing Tiers:** Raw data is $7M+/yr (due to competitive risk); aggregated data is $100k–$999k/yr, with price varying by scope.](https://fathom.video/share/w24K97Mf6mHqpviLmPa_iufLHuVyr2ZQ?tab=summary&timestamp=258.0)
  - [**Strategic Decision:** GrowthX must decide whether to augment its current data or replace it entirely, weighing the cost, implementation time, and legal complexity of each option.](https://fathom.video/share/w24K97Mf6mHqpviLmPa_iufLHuVyr2ZQ?tab=summary&timestamp=643.0)

## Topics

### Similarweb's AI Prompt Data

  - [**Source:** Data is collected from a panel of real users, not via API probing.](https://fathom.video/share/w24K97Mf6mHqpviLmPa_iufLHuVyr2ZQ?tab=summary&timestamp=113.0)
  - [**Volume:**](https://fathom.video/share/w24K97Mf6mHqpviLmPa_iufLHuVyr2ZQ?tab=summary&timestamp=909.0)
      - [**Global:** \~80M prompts/mo (includes code blocks).](https://fathom.video/share/w24K97Mf6mHqpviLmPa_iufLHuVyr2ZQ?tab=summary&timestamp=909.0)
      - [**US English:** \~8M prompts/mo (excludes code blocks).](https://fathom.video/share/w24K97Mf6mHqpviLmPa_iufLHuVyr2ZQ?tab=summary&timestamp=909.0)
  - [**Engine Coverage:**](https://fathom.video/share/w24K97Mf6mHqpviLmPa_iufLHuVyr2ZQ?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)
      - [**Primary:** ChatGPT (\~70% of volume), Gemini.](https://fathom.video/share/w24K97Mf6mHqpviLmPa_iufLHuVyr2ZQ?tab=summary&timestamp=455.0)
      - [**Other:** Perplexity.](https://fathom.video/share/w24K97Mf6mHqpviLmPa_iufLHuVyr2ZQ?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)
      - [**In Progress:** Claude.](https://fathom.video/share/w24K97Mf6mHqpviLmPa_iufLHuVyr2ZQ?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)
      - [**Not Covered:** Google AI Overviews (requires a dedicated client to fund the storage).](https://fathom.video/share/w24K97Mf6mHqpviLmPa_iufLHuVyr2ZQ?tab=summary&timestamp=368.0)

### Partnership Options & Pricing

  - [**Custom Engagements:** Delivery is via custom data science projects, not a standard API.](https://fathom.video/share/w24K97Mf6mHqpviLmPa_iufLHuVyr2ZQ?tab=summary&timestamp=207.0)
  - [**Pricing Tiers (per year):**](https://fathom.video/share/w24K97Mf6mHqpviLmPa_iufLHuVyr2ZQ?tab=summary&timestamp=258.0)
      - [**Raw Data:** $7M+](https://fathom.video/share/w24K97Mf6mHqpviLmPa_iufLHuVyr2ZQ?tab=summary&timestamp=258.0)
          - [**Rationale:** High price reflects the competitive risk of licensing raw data.](https://fathom.video/share/w24K97Mf6mHqpviLmPa_iufLHuVyr2ZQ?tab=summary&timestamp=258.0)
          - [**Implementation:** Requires a \~6-month modeling cycle.](https://fathom.video/share/w24K97Mf6mHqpviLmPa_iufLHuVyr2ZQ?tab=summary&timestamp=612.0)
          - [**Legal:** More complex to approve.](https://fathom.video/share/w24K97Mf6mHqpviLmPa_iufLHuVyr2ZQ?tab=summary&timestamp=752.0)
      - [**Aggregated Data:** $100k–$999k](https://fathom.video/share/w24K97Mf6mHqpviLmPa_iufLHuVyr2ZQ?tab=summary&timestamp=258.0)
          - [**Rationale:** Lower cost and faster time-to-market.](https://fathom.video/share/w24K97Mf6mHqpviLmPa_iufLHuVyr2ZQ?tab=summary&timestamp=589.0)
          - [**Implementation:** Similarweb models the data, saving GrowthX dev cycles.](https://fathom.video/share/w24K97Mf6mHqpviLmPa_iufLHuVyr2ZQ?tab=summary&timestamp=589.0)
          - [**Legal:** Simpler to approve (saves \~3 months).](https://fathom.video/share/w24K97Mf6mHqpviLmPa_iufLHuVyr2ZQ?tab=summary&timestamp=752.0)

### Licensing & Approval Process

  - [**Requirement:** C-suite approval is mandatory due to Similarweb's own AI product.](https://fathom.video/share/w24K97Mf6mHqpviLmPa_iufLHuVyr2ZQ?tab=summary&timestamp=545.0)
  - [**Key Approval Questions:**](https://fathom.video/share/w24K97Mf6mHqpviLmPa_iufLHuVyr2ZQ?tab=summary&timestamp=551.0)
      - [**Use Case:** Augment current data vs. replace it?](https://fathom.video/share/w24K97Mf6mHqpviLmPa_iufLHuVyr2ZQ?tab=summary&timestamp=643.0)
      - [**Delivery:** Raw vs. aggregated data?](https://fathom.video/share/w24K97Mf6mHqpviLmPa_iufLHuVyr2ZQ?tab=summary&timestamp=589.0)
      - [**Integration:** Front-end (displaying prompts to users) vs. back-end?](https://fathom.video/share/w24K97Mf6mHqpviLmPa_iufLHuVyr2ZQ?tab=summary&timestamp=570.0)
          - [**Note:** Front-end use of raw prompts is unlikely to be approved due to security and privacy concerns.](https://fathom.video/share/w24K97Mf6mHqpviLmPa_iufLHuVyr2ZQ?tab=summary&timestamp=570.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Rob:** Email Stevie the C-suite approval questions.](https://fathom.video/share/w24K97Mf6mHqpviLmPa_iufLHuVyr2ZQ?tab=summary&timestamp=551.0)
  - [**Stevie:** Review the proposal with the CTO and CEO to define the strategic approach (augment vs. replace).](https://fathom.video/share/w24K97Mf6mHqpviLmPa_iufLHuVyr2ZQ?tab=summary&timestamp=643.0)
  - [**Stevie:** Schedule a follow-up call in a few weeks with the CTO and a Similarweb data expert.](https://fathom.video/share/w24K97Mf6mHqpviLmPa_iufLHuVyr2ZQ?tab=summary&timestamp=773.0)

action_items.description: Email Stevie C-suite Qs re: use case, front/back, raw/modeled, scope, insights
Email Rob C-suite approval info; then brief CTO/CEO, decide approach, schedule follow-up w/ Rob + expert
